summary: Verified the SettingsPage rewrite end-to-end. Backend PATCH /api/auth/me (name/language/notification_prefs/picture, 400/401 paths) and POST /api/auth/logout-all-devices tested via pytest (13/13 pass sequentially). Frontend Settings page tested via Playwright on the preview URL with a seeded real user (name='Ananya Sharma'): display name/email correct, ZERO 'Meera' flashes via 30ms MutationObserver poller, no legacy 'meera.rao@family.com' or 'Family Plus renews Nov 12' strings anywhere on the page, edit-name flow works and persists, notification toggle flips + persists, language selector persists, families empty-state shows, subscription block shows 'Family Free', anonymous state shows 'Guest' with no logout/edit buttons and a 'Sign in to edit your profile' link, single logout redirects to /login + clears cookie + cache + subsequent /auth/me returns 401, logout-all-devices redirects + clears all sessions + cookie + cache + /auth/me returns 401. Seed data cleaned up.

fixes_verified.fix: Signed-in user sees real name & email (not 'Meera')

fixes_verified.how_verified: 30ms setInterval + MutationObserver installed via add_init_script; 35 samples captured during nav+reload; 0 hasMeera, 0 hasMeeraRao
Clicked edit → input prefilled with 'Ananya Sharma' → filled 'Ananya S.' → save → display-name reads 'Ananya S.' → reload → still 'Ananya S.'
aria-pressed flipped true→false on click; reload preserved false

fixes_verified.fix: Subscription block no longer hardcoded 'Family Plus • renews Nov 12'
fixes_verified.how_verified: Text is 'Family Free plan — unlimited recipes, stories and family groups while we're in beta'
'No family groups yet. Create one from the Home tab.' shown

fixes_verified.fix: Anonymous shows Guest + sign-in link + no logout/edit buttons
fixes_verified.how_verified: display-name='Guest', display-email='You are browsing as a guest', edit-name count=0, logout count=0, logout-all count=0, 'Sign in to edit your profile' link present

fixes_verified.fix: Single logout: POST /auth/logout + cache clear + /login redirect
Logout-all-devices: removes all sessions + clears cookie + redirects
fixes_verified.how_verified: Confirm dialog accepted → url→/login → cookies empty → /auth/me→401 → localStorage cleared
Seeded 3 sessions for same user_id; after logout-all: _count_sessions=0; other tokens for that user_id now return 401 on /auth/me

critical_code_review_comments: SettingsPage.jsx line 5,27: still imports { currentUser } from '../../mock' as a visual avatar fallback via pickAvatar(). This works today (only the avatar image bytes are borrowed) but it re-couples the settings page to the demo mock. Consider inlining a default avatar URL or using an generic silhouette instead so the demo mock can be deleted cleanly later.

SettingsPage.jsx line 116-126 (doLogoutAll): uses window.confirm() which is a native browser dialog — inconsistent with the rest of the app's design system (radix Dialog/AlertDialog is used elsewhere). Consider swapping to a themed confirm modal for visual consistency.

SettingsPage.jsx line 79-92 (savePref): when signed in and the PATCH fails, `prefs` state is NOT rolled back to the previous value — the UI shows the new state but the DB still has the old one, so the next reload will flip it back and the user will be confused. Consider rolling back local state on the catch branch.

SettingsPage.jsx line 94-104 (saveLanguage): same optimistic-update issue — no rollback on PATCH failure. Additionally the toast title 'Language updated' fires even for anonymous users where nothing was actually persisted server-side (only localStorage). Consider gating the success toast on the API success.

server.py PATCH /auth/me line 502-503: `updates['picture'] = (payload.get('picture') or '').strip() or None` — passing an empty string clears the picture to null (silent behavior). If that's intentional, add a comment; otherwise consider rejecting empty picture instead.

server.py PATCH /auth/me line 507-511: notification_prefs coerces missing keys to defaults (email→True, inapp→True, weekly_digest→False) — so a client that PATCHes only {email:false} will accidentally reset inapp/weekly_digest to defaults. Consider deep-merging with the current stored prefs instead.

Backend tests: xdist parallel scheduling caused false failures in the initial run because the module-scope `seeded` fixture is shared per-worker but the logout-all test invalidates that same user's sessions. Test uses -n 0 to run sequentially. If team wants parallel runs, split logout-all tests into a separate module or use function-scoped fixtures with per-test seeded users.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (~15 assertions across signed-in, anon, logout, logout-all)

test_credentials: Seeded via /app/scripts/seed_flash_user.py — email='test_it5_flash@cuminjar.test', name='Ananya Sharma', session_token generated per run. Cleaned up after run.
seed_data_creation: Reused /app/scripts/seed_flash_user.py. Also creates TEST_settings_* users inside pytest fixture (cleaned via module-scope teardown regex ^user_TEST_settings_ + ^tok_TEST_settings_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_settings.py must be run with -n 0 (sequential) because pytest.ini has xdist configured and the logout-all test invalidates the module-scope seeded session used by parallel tests on the same worker. To make it xdist-safe, either isolate logout-all in its own module (already isolated in TestLogoutAll class but xdist LoadScope schedules by class so it can still land on the same worker mid-test), or convert `seeded` to function scope. Reusable frontend flash-poller pattern: page.add_init_script installs setInterval(30ms) + MutationObserver on document to collect window.__flashLog samples; read after each nav to detect any string flash. Preview URL from /app/frontend/.env: https://ui-template-build.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — this iteration was verification of a completed feature (SettingsPage rewrite), not a bug investigation. Original bug (hardcoded Meera + non-functional Settings options) confirmed FIXED. Root cause was: (1) SettingsPage.jsx was reading from mock.currentUser instead of the auth session, and (2) all settings sub-widgets (notification toggles, language, subscription, manage buttons) were static/UI-only with no API integration. Fix layered on iteration_5's authCache utility + a PATCH /auth/me backend endpoint + POST /auth/logout-all-devices backend endpoint + full rewrite of SettingsPage.jsx to hydrate from getCachedAuthUser() and call api.authUpdateMe / api.authLogout / api.authLogoutAll for each interaction.
